lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: And tonight, before the dessert course, we present for your pleasure a traditional cheese trolley.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: To start, we have an excellent clochette. Clearly very nice, very light.

lines.speaker: Remy
lines.text: This is me. I think it's apparent that I need to rethink my life a little bit.

lines.speaker: Remy
lines.text: I can't help myself. I like good food, okay? And good food is hard for a rat to find.

lines.speaker: Django
lines.text: It wouldn't be so hard to find if you weren't so picky.

lines.speaker: Remy
lines.text: I don't want to eat garbage, Dad!

lines.speaker: Django
lines.text: What is that?

lines.speaker: Remy
lines.text: I don't really know.

lines.speaker: Django
lines.text: You don't know, and you're eating it?

lines.speaker: Remy
lines.text: You know, if you can sort of muscle your way past the gag reflex, all kinds of food possibilities open up.

lines.speaker: Remy
lines.text: I don't think any of this would have come up, but we happen to live in Paris, France. And it's so easy to find good food in Paris — it's just dangerous.

lines.speaker: Django
lines.text: Gotta rethink your life.

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: Mama always said life was like a box of chocolates. You never know what you're gonna get.

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: I just felt like running.

lines.speaker: Jenny
lines.text: Run, Forrest! Run!

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: That day, for no particular reason, I decided to go for a little run.

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: I ran to the end of the road, and when I got there I thought maybe I would run to the end of town.

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: When I got tired, I slept. When I got hungry, I ate. When I had to go, you know, I went.

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: I think I'll go home now. I'm pretty tired. I think I'll just walk.

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: Mama always said, dying was a part of life. I sure wish it wasn't.

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: Jenny taught me how to read, and I taught her how to climb.

lines.speaker: Forrest
lines.text: I wrote you every day for a year.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Mr. Navorski, please follow me.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: While you were in the air, there was a military coup in your country. Republic of Krakozhia is under new leadership.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: I don't think he gets it.

lines.speaker: Viktor
lines.text: What do I buy the Nike shoes? I'm gonna need the passport also.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: No, no. Mr. Navorski, beyond those doors is American soil. You are not to leave this building.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: There's a man walking around the terminal in a bathrobe.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: That guy doesn't even speak English.

lines.speaker: Enrique
lines.text: She's a wild stallion. You help me to win her heart, and you'll never go hungry again.

lines.speaker: Viktor
lines.text: We headed for home. I am delayed a long time.

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: You are the kind of woman who can get any guy she wants.

lines.speaker: Viktor
lines.text: Why, Viktor Navorski, that's something a guy like you could never understand.

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: Do you ever feel like you're just living in an airport?

lines.speaker: Marlin
lines.text: Dory, do you see anything?

lines.speaker: Dory
lines.text: Something's got me! That was me. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Dory
lines.text: I see a light! What is it? It's so pretty. I'm feeling happy, which is a big deal for me.

lines.speaker: Dory
lines.text: I want to touch it! Hey, come back!

lines.speaker: Dory
lines.text: I'm going to get you! I'm going to swim with you! I'm going to be your best friend!

lines.speaker: Marlin
lines.text: I got to find my son. Nemo!

lines.speaker: Bruce
lines.text: Hello! Fish are friends, not food.

lines.speaker: Crush
lines.text: Grab shell, dude!

lines.speaker: Crush
lines.text: No hurling on the shell, dude. Okay? Just waxed it.

lines.speaker: Dory
lines.text: How's it going, Bob? You know, I speak whale.

lines.speaker: Dory
lines.text: Don't worry. Whales don't eat clownfish. They eat krill.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: When the McCallister family left on their Christmas vacation, they forgot one small thing.

lines.speaker: Mom
lines.text: Did you lock up? Did we set the timers on the lights?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: In the northern suburbs, police are on the lookout for a pair of burglars who are calling themselves the Wet Bandits.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: You know that you're in there! It's Santa Claus and his elf! Get off my property!

lines.speaker: Kevin
lines.text: This is my house. I have to defend it.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Where's your mother?

lines.speaker: Kevin
lines.text: My mom's in the car.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Where's your father?

lines.speaker: Kevin
lines.text: He's at work.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: He's a kid. What can a kid do to us?

lines.speaker: Marv
lines.text: Kids are stupid.

lines.speaker: Mom
lines.text: I don't care if I have to get out on your runway and hitchhike. I am going to get home to my son.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Yesterday he was just a kid, but tonight he's a home security system.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: A family comedy without the family. Home Alone. Coming November 16th.

lines.speaker: Kevin
lines.text: I'm eight years old. You think I'd be here all alone? I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Summer in the city of Arendelle. It couldn't be warmer. It couldn't be sunnier. But that's about to change forever.

lines.speaker: Kristoff
lines.text: Arendelle — it's completely frozen!

lines.speaker: Kristoff
lines.text: A real howler in July. The land is covered in eternal snow.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: If we don't do something soon, we'll all freeze to death.

lines.speaker: Kristoff
lines.text: You want to talk about a problem? I sell ice for a living. That's a rough business to be in right now.

lines.speaker: Olaf
lines.text: Hi, everyone! I'm Olaf.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: You're creepy.

lines.speaker: Olaf
lines.text: Well, we got off to a bad start.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: I know how to stop this winter.

lines.speaker: Kristoff
lines.text: Get your feet down! This is fresh lacquer. Seriously, were you raised in a barn?

lines.speaker: Olaf
lines.text: Some people are worth melting for. Just maybe not right this second.

lines.speaker: Olaf
lines.text: My legs! I can't get my legs! Those are my legs!

lines.speaker: Olaf
lines.text: Oh hey, do me a favor, grab my butt. Oh, that feels better.

movieTitle: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone

lines.speaker: Hagrid
lines.text: You're a wizard, Harry. You'll be the finest student Hogwarts has ever seen.

lines.speaker: McGonagall
lines.text: Welcome, student. This is your classroom.

lines.speaker: Hermione
lines.text: I'm going to the library to study. I've read about it in a book.

lines.speaker: Ron
lines.text: She needs to sort out her priorities. Homework and study all the time!

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: My teacher says I have a special gift.

lines.speaker: Dumbledore
lines.text: It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live. Every student must learn this.

lines.speaker: Hermione
lines.text: Now, if you two don't mind, I'm going to the classroom before either of you come up with another clever idea to get us killed.

lines.speaker: Ron
lines.text: She's a nightmare, honestly. No wonder she's got no friends. Always doing homework!

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: I'm not going home. Hogwarts is my home. Best classroom and teacher I've ever had.

lines.speaker: Dumbledore
lines.text: The truth. It is a beautiful and terrible thing, and should therefore be treated with great caution. Your exam of character matters most.

lines.speaker: Andy
lines.text: I have an interview with Miranda Priestly for a meeting at Runway magazine.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: She's not happy unless everyone is panicking. Send the email now!

lines.speaker: Miranda
lines.text: By all means, move at a glacial pace. You know how that thrills me. The deadline was yesterday.

lines.speaker: Andy
lines.text: I am smart. I work very hard on every project they give me.

lines.speaker: Miranda
lines.text: That's all. Details of your incompetence do not interest me. Tell the manager.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I love my job. I love my job. My colleague better not mess this up.

lines.speaker: Andy
lines.text: My personal life is hanging by a thread. Every project has a crazy deadline.

lines.speaker: Miranda
lines.text: Every meeting must start on time. No excuses.

lines.speaker: Nigel
lines.text: Let me know when your whole life goes up in smoke. That means it's time for a meeting with Nigel.

lines.speaker: Andy
lines.text: I learned so much from this job. Miranda is the most demanding manager in fashion.
